title: lupinus arcticus var. cottonii
intro: Lupinus arcticus var. cottonii, also known as Arctic Lupine, is a subspecies of the Lupinus arcticus plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lupinus arcticus var. cottonii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from pain and discomfort. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
